title: Warming Squash Soup with Sesame Cheese Cracker
summary: In Partnership With Aldi. #ad A silky, spicy squash soup with a crispy, nutty cheddar cracker you smash at the table. Big heat, bold flavour, and a bit of drama too.

method.description: Peel the squash and cut it into 3cm chunks. Peel the onion and cut into wedges. Peel the garlic cloves but keep them whole. Halve the chillies lengthways and remove the seeds if you prefer less heat. Trim the spring onions for roasting. Peel and thinly slice the ginger.

Add the squash, onion, garlic and chillies to a roasting tin. Drizzle with olive oil, season with salt and pepper, and toss everything together. Roast for 25–30 mins, turning once, until golden and soft.
Add the sliced ginger and trimmed spring onions to the tin, toss again, and roast for another 10 mins.

Tip all the roasted veg into a large saucepan. Pour in the coconut milk and vegetable stock. Stir in the ground turmeric and 1 Tbsp soy sauce. Bring to the boil and simmer gently for 10 mins. Taste and add more soy sauce, if needed.

While the soup simmers, make your sesame cheddar cracker. Grate the cheddar. Heat a non-stick frying pan over a medium heat. Add roughly 50g grated cheddar to the pan in a mound. Let it melt and bubble for 4–5 mins until starting to brown.Sprinkle over some sesame seeds and a pinch of chilli flakes. Keep cooking for another minute or so, until it darkens but doesn’t burn. Carefully lift the cheese from the pan using a spatula and transfer to a tray to crisp up. Repeat with the remaining cheese.
Use a stick blender to blitz the soup until smooth. If it’s too thick, loosen with a splash of hot water or extra stock. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Finely slice the spring onions for garnish. Ladle the soup into bowls, top with the sliced spring onions, and serve with a cheese cracker on top, smash it at the table.

chefs.name: Ayala Daly
chefs.summary: Ayala Daly is Mob's content producer and resident vibe bringer. You’ve probably seen her all over the stories but she’s no stranger to the kitchen. Ayala blends her background as a food researcher with her passion for cooking. With a knack for experimentation, she crafts easy-to-follow recipes bursting with bold flavours, drawing inspiration from regular trips to her favourite farms and the thrill of all things spicy.
